text: Cosine annealing is a learning rate schedule that smoothly decreases the learning rate following a cosine curve.
The formula combines the minimum and maximum learning rates using a cosine function based on the current training step within each cycle.
Watch the cosine curve being drawn, showing how the learning rate decays smoothly from maximum to minimum.
The schedule is divided into cycles, with each cycle representing one full cosine period.
Within each cycle, the learning rate oscillates high to low, allowing the model to make both large and fine-grained updates.
The warm restarts periodically bring the learning rate back to maximum, helping escape local minima.
Key benefits include smooth decay, periodic restarts, and escaping local minima in optimization.
